sections.title: 1. "N+1 query" equivalent — none, but watch the O(n) array scans
2. "Unnecessary re-renders" equivalent — full list teardown on every action
3. Caching opportunity — counts are computed twice per render
4. Memory leaks — none found
sections.content:
I checked the usual suspects:
- No `setInterval`/`setTimeout` left running.
- The one global listener (`window.addEventListener("storage", ...)`, app.js:233) is registered once for the app's lifetime — expected, not a leak.
- Per-item listeners (`checkBtn`, `text`, `deleteBtn`) are attached to nodes that get fully discarded via `innerHTML = ""` on every render — when a node with no other references is removed from the DOM, it (and its listeners) becomes garbage-collectable. So this isn't a leak, just wasteful churn (covered in #2 above).

No actual memory leak risk in this codebase.

sections.title: 5. Redundant computations — `filteredTodos()` and counts recomputed per render
sections.content:
Same root cause as #3: every call to `render()` re-derives `filteredTodos()`, `activeCount`, and `completedCount` from `state.todos` even when only one item changed. For a list this small it's invisible to the user, but if you adopt the targeted-patch approach in #2, you'd also stop recomputing the full filtered list on actions that don't affect the current filter view.

---

sections.title: One unrelated thing I noticed
sections.content:
There's an untracked file in the working directory literally named `deleteTodo(todo.id))` (visible in `git status`). It looks like a stray artifact — possibly text accidentally saved as a filename rather than code. Want me to remove it, or is it intentional?

**Bottom line:** the app is small enough that none of this is urgent. If you want, I can apply the event-delegation/targeted-render fix (#2) since it's the only change with a visible effect (stops the re-animation glitch on every toggle) — let me know.

rawOutputPreview:
This is a vanilla JS/HTML/CSS to-do app (`app.js`, `index.html`, `styles.css` — no React, no backend, no database). So two of the five categories don't literally apply here — there's no SQL/API layer (no N+1 queries) and no React (no component re-renders). I'll cover the vanilla-JS equivalents of those plus the categories that do apply, all grounded in the actual code.

## 1. "N+1 query" equivalent — none, but watch the O(n) array scans

There's no database, just `state.todos` in memory + `localStorage`. The closest analog: `updateTodo` (app.js:64-66) does a full `.map()` over every todo to update one by `id`, and `toggleTodo`/`deleteTodo` similarly scan the whole array. At today's scale (a personal to-do list) this is irrelevant, but if the list ever grows large, lookups become O(n) per action instead of O(1):

```js
// Current: O(n) scan + O(n) array rebuild for a single update
function updateTodo(id, changes) {
  state.todos = state.todos.map((todo) => (todo.id === id ? { ...todo, ...changes } : todo));
}
```

Not worth fixing for this app's actual size — flagging only because it's the literal answer to "N+1-style" scans.

## 2. "Unnecessary re-renders" equivalent — full list teardown on every action

This is the real, actionable finding. `render()` (app.js:110-137) does `refs.todoList.innerHTML = ""` then rebuilds **every** todo item from scratch — on every single toggle, add, delete, or filter change. Consequences:

- Every event listener on every item is destroyed and recreated each time (app.js:149,156-159,168).
- The entrance animation (`requestAnimationFrame(() => item.classList.add("visible"))`, app.js:171) replays for **all** items, not just the one that changed — toggling one checkbox visibly re-animates the entire list.
- Editing input focus/selection is lost if something else triggers `sync()` mid-edit.

**Fix — event delegation + targeted DOM patch instead of full rebuild:**

```js
// Attach once, not per-item
refs.todoList.addEventListener("click", (
